titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 600

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=938 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 12:40:59.75782+00 | translation moa=939 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 12:40:59.747406+00

p.cl: . W’ . dei Gratia Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis Hominibus Tocius Terre sue . Clericis et laicis  Salutem .

Sciant presentes et futuri . me dedisse . et Concessisse . et Hac Carta mea Confirmasse . Roberto . de
Aubein’ . et heredibus suis . illas viginti . marcas . que mihi debentur annuatim de Waitinga quam Prior
et Conuentus de Coldingham . mihi debent de terris suis de Coldingham . donec ego uel heredes mei
eidem Roberto . uel heredibus suis dederimus . viginti . marcatis . terre . ex australi parte maris
Scott’ . uel inter le Muneth . et mare Scott’ . Quare uolo ut predictus Robertus . de Aubein’ . et here
des sui illas viginti . marcas percipiant singulis annis ad pascha . et precipio priori de Colding
ham . et bailliuis eius . quatinus annuatim sine inpedimento illas viginti . marcas eis reddant ad pas
cha  donec ego uel heredes mei eis dederimus predictas viginti . marcatas terre .

Testibus . Alexandro .
filio meo . Willelmo . de Boscho . Cancellario . meo . Philippo . de Valoniis . Camerario . meo . Iohanne de Maccuswel’ . Ma
gistro Martino . medico meo . Waltero . Cumin’ . Iordano . Cumin’ .
William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

Let present and future know me to have given, conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to Robert de Aubein and his heirs the twenty merks which he owed me annually for 'waiting' which the prior and convent of Coldingham owe me for their lands of Coldingham, until I or my heirs shall have given to the same Robert or his heirs twenty merks of land on the south side of the Scottish Sea [the Forth], or between the Mounth and the Scottish Sea. Wherefore I will that the said Robert de Aubein and his heirs receive those twenty merks each year at Easter and I instruct the prior of Coldingham and his baillies that they annually render the twenty merks to them without impediment at Easter untill I or my heirs shall have given the twenty merks of land.
As witnesses: Alexander, my son; William del Bois, my chancellor; Philip de Valognes, my chamberlain; John of Maxwell; Master Martin, my physician; Walter Comyn; Jordan Comyn.
At Selkirk on the 28th day of February.
